kind: dense data-model + bulk row-copy for Pad
lever_summary: Pad built a Vec<Literal> (per-element Literal fill + per-element odometer placement). Dense path (F64/I64): typed dense fill, then bulk copy_from_slice of whole contiguous input rows when no-interior/no-crop (pad_copy_rows, O(rows)), else per-element placement (pad_fill_place); dense output via new_*_values.
method: same-bench A/B; baseline = stashed generic Pad; normalized by eval/sort_calib_reduce_64k_i64 (ReduceSum, unaffected).

change.lever: eval_pad: edge cases first; dense F64/I64 path with row_copyable check -> pad_copy_rows (bulk copy_from_slice) else pad_fill_place; dense output. Added eval/pad_256x256_to_258x258_f64 bench + pad_dense_matches_literal_path test (crop/interior + no-crop row-copy).

isomorphism.type: BIT-IDENTICAL: pad_copy_rows places identical elements at identical row-major offsets (no-interior/no-crop), pad_fill_place mirrors the generic odometer for crop/interior; dense output value-equal
isomorphism.validation: pad_dense_matches_literal_path (f64/i64, both paths) + 23 pad tests pass

note: First measured a simple input-only dense variant (kept per-element placement) at ~1.40x; the win is the bulk ROW COPY (256 copy_from_slice vs 65536 per-element placements + Literal fill/output). Dense vein now covers reductions/cumulative/argmax/elementwise/convert/broadcast/pad.
